key_points: The poem 'A Concrete Example' describes a miniature garden owned by Mrs. Jones
The garden contains very tiny features such as a crazy path, rockery, lily pond and sundial
The narrator visits the garden and listens to Mrs. Jones proudly describing its features
Because everything is extremely small, the narrator struggles to see the flower she mentions
The poem ends humorously when the narrator realizes he is standing on the tiny flower
examples: The crazy path represents a small winding path in the garden
The lily pond is a tiny water feature decorated with lilies
The rockery is made of small stones arranged artistically
The sundial is a miniature object used to tell time in the garden
The tiny flower under the narrator’s feet creates the humorous twist in the poem
literary_elements: Imagery → The poem uses detailed descriptions to help readers imagine the miniature garden
Hyperbole → The smallness of the garden objects is exaggerated for effect
Humor → The poem ends with a surprising and amusing twist
Descriptive language → The poet carefully describes each element of the garden
exam_tips: Remember the setting of the poem – Mrs. Jones’s miniature garden
Understand how exaggeration and description create humor
Focus on the narrator’s confusion about the tiny flower
Know the message: small details in life are often overlooked but can be interesting
